pkill -f "next dev"
sleep 2
cd ~/Empire/founder_dashboard && npm run dev > ~/Empire/logs/dashboard.log 2>&1 &
sleep 5
echo "Dashboard restarted! Refresh http://localhost:3000"


The paste got corrupted. Let's do it with nano instead:

nano ~/Empire/founder_dashboard/src/app/page.tsx


Then select all (Ctrl+Shift+A) and delete everything. Then paste this complete file (copy the entire block below, then paste into nano):

TSX
'use client';

import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from 'react';

const API_URL = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://localhost:8000/api/v1';

interface Message {
  role: 'user' | 'assistant';
  content: string;
  model?: string;
}

interface Desk {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  status: string;
  stats: { completed: number; failed: number };
}

interface AIModel {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  available: boolean;
  type: string;
}

interface SystemStatus {
  backend: string;
  ollama: string;
  internet: boolean;
}

export default function FounderDashboard() {
  const [messages, setMessages] = useState<Message[]>([
    { role: 'assistant', content: "Hello! I'm MAX, your AI Assistant Manager. All 8 desks are online. How can I help?" }
  ]);
  const [input, setInput] = useState('');
  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false);
  const [desks, setDesks] = useState<Desk[]>([]);
  const [models, setModels] = useState<AIModel[]>([]);
  const [selectedModel, setSelectedModel] = useState('claude');
  const [stats, setStats] = useState({ total_completed: 0, active_tasks: 0 });
  const [systemStatus, setSystemStatus] = useState<SystemStatus>({ backend: '...', ollama: '...', internet: true });
  const messagesEndRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchDesks();
    fetchModels();
    fetchStats();
    checkSystemStatus();
    const interval = setInterval(() => { fetchDesks(); fetchStats(); checkSystemStatus(); }, 30000);
    return () => clearInterval(interval);
  }, []);

  useEffect(() => {
    messagesEndRef.current?.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth' });
  }, [messages]);

  const fetchDesks = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(API_URL + '/max/desks');
      const data = await res.json();
      setDesks(data.desks || []);
    } catch (e) { console.error('Failed to fetch desks:', e); }
  };

  const fetchModels = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(API_URL + '/max/models');
      const data = await res.json();
      setModels(data.models || []);
    } catch (e) { console.error('Failed to fetch models:', e); }
  };

  const fetchStats = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(API_URL + '/max/stats');
      const data = await res.json();
      setStats(data.stats || {});
    } catch (e) { console.error('Failed to fetch stats:', e); }
  };

  const checkSystemStatus = async () => {
    try {
      const backendRes = await fetch(API_URL.replace('/api/v1', '') + '/health');
      const backendOk = backendRes.ok ? '200' : 'ERR';
      let ollamaOk = 'OFF';
      try {
        const ollamaRes = await fetch('http://localhost:11434/api/tags');
        ollamaOk = ollamaRes.ok ? '200' : 'ERR';
      } catch { ollamaOk = 'OFF'; }
      setSystemStatus({ backend: backendOk, ollama: ollamaOk, internet: true });
    } catch (e) {
      setSystemStatus({ backend: 'ERR', ollama: 'ERR', internet: false });
    }
  };

  const sendMessage = async () => {
    if (!input.trim() || isLoading) return;
    const userMessage: Message = { role: 'user', content: input };
    setMessages(prev => [...prev, userMessage]);
    setInput('');
    setIsLoading(true);
    try {
      const res = await fetch(API_URL + '/max/chat', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ message: input, model: selectedModel, history: messages.map(m => ({ role: m.role, content: m.content })) })
      });
      const data = await res.json();
      const modelUsed = data.model_used || selectedModel;
      const isOffline = modelUsed.includes('ollama');
      if (isOffline && systemStatus.internet) {
        setSystemStatus(prev => ({ ...prev, internet: false }));
      }
      setMessages(prev => [...prev, { role: 'assistant', content: data.response || 'Error processing request.', model: modelUsed }]);
    } catch (e) {
      setMessages(prev => [...prev, { role: 'assistant', content: 'Connection error. Check if backend is running.' }]);
    } finally { setIsLoading(false); }
  };

  const getStatusColor = (status: string) => status === 'idle' ? 'bg-green-500' : status === 'busy' ? 'bg-yellow-500' : 'bg-gray-500';
  const getStatusDot = (status: string) => status === '200' ? 'bg-green-500' : status === 'OFF' ? 'bg-yellow-500' : 'bg-red-500';

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen bg-[#050508] flex">
      <div className="w-72 bg-[#0a0a0f] border-r border-gray-800 flex flex-col">
        <div className="p-4 border-b border-gray-800">
          <h1 className="text-xl font-bold text-purple-400">EmpireBox</h1>
          <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">Founder Edition</p>
        </div>
        <div className="p-3 border-b border-gray-800">
          <h3 className="text-xs font-semibold text-gray-500 uppercase mb-2">System Status</h3>
          <div className="space-y-1 text-xs">
            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
              <span className="text-gray-400">Backend</span>
              <div className="flex items-center gap-1"><div className={`w-2 h-2 rounded-full ${getStatusDot(systemStatus.backend)}`} /><span className="text-gray-500">{systemStatus.backend}</span></div>
            </div>
            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
              <span className="text-gray-400">Ollama</span>
              <div className="flex items-center gap-1"><div className={`w-2 h-2 rounded-full ${getStatusDot(systemStatus.ollama)}`} /><span className="text-gray-500">{systemStatus.ollama}</span></div>
            </div>
            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
              <span className="text-gray-400">Internet</span>
              <div className="flex items-center gap-1"><div className={`w-2 h-2 rounded-full ${systemStatus.internet ? 'bg-green-500' : 'bg-red-500'}`} /><span className="text-gray-500">{systemStatus.internet ? 'Online' : 'Offline'}</span></div>
            </div>
          </div>
          {!systemStatus.internet && <div className="mt-2 p-2 bg-yellow-900/30 border border-yellow-700 rounded text-xs text-yellow-400">Using Ollama fallback</div>}
        </div>
        <div className="p-3 grid grid-cols-2 gap-2">
          <div className="bg-[#050508] rounded-lg p-3 border border-gray-800"><span className="text-xs text-gray-400">Completed</span><p className="text-xl font-bold text-white">{stats.total_completed}</p></div>
          <div className="bg-[#050508] rounded-lg p-3 border border-gray-800"><span className="text-xs text-gray-400">Active</span><p className="text-xl font-bold text-white">{stats.active_tasks}</p></div>
        </div>
        <div className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-3">
          <h3 className="text-xs font-semibold text-gray-500 uppercase mb-2 px-2">AI Desks</h3>
          {desks.map(desk => (
            <div key={desk.id} className="flex items-center gap-3 p-2 rounded-lg hover:bg-[#050508] cursor-pointer">
              <div className="relative">
                <div className="w-8 h-8 rounded-lg bg-gray-800 flex items-center justify-center text-gray-400 text-sm">{desk.name.charAt(0)}</div>
                <div className={`absolute -bottom-0.5 -right-0.5 w-2.5 h-2.5 rounded-full border-2 border-[#0a0a0f] ${getStatusColor(desk.status)}`} />
              </div>
              <div className="flex-1"><p className="text-sm font-medium text-gray-200">{desk.name}</p><p className="text-xs text-gray-500">{desk.description}</p></div>
            </div>
          ))}
        </div>
      </div>
      <div className="flex-1 flex flex-col">
        <div className="h-14 bg-[#0a0a0f] border-b border-gray-800 flex items-center justify-between px-4">
          <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
            <div className="w-8 h-8 rounded-full bg-purple-600 flex items-center justify-center text-white font-bold">M</div>
            <div><h2 className="font-semibold text-white">MAX</h2><p className="text-xs text-gray-400">AI Assistant Manager</p></div>
          </div>
          <select value={selectedModel} onChange={(e) => setSelectedModel(e.target.value)} className="bg-[#050508] border border-gray-700 rounded-lg px-3 py-1.5 text-sm text-gray-300">
            {models.map(m => (<option key={m.id} value={m.id} disabled={!m.available}>{m.name} {!m.available && '(No Key)'}</option>))}
          </select>
        </div>
        <div className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-4 space-y-4">
          {messages.map((msg, i) => (
            <div key={i} className={`flex ${msg.role === 'user' ? 'justify-end' : 'justify-start'}`}>
              <div className={`max-w-[80%] rounded-2xl px-4 py-3 ${msg.role === 'user' ? 'bg-purple-600 text-white' : 'bg-[#0a0a0f] border border-gray-800 text-gray-200'}`}>
                <p className="whitespace-pre-wrap">{msg.content}</p>
                {msg.model && <p className="text-xs mt-2 opacity-50">via {msg.model} {msg.model.includes('ollama') ? '🏠' : '☁️'}</p>}
              </div>
            </div>
          ))}
          {isLoading && <div className="flex justify-start"><div className="bg-[#0a0a0f] border border-gray-800 rounded-2xl px-4 py-3 text-gray-400">Thinking...</div></div>}
          <div ref={messagesEndRef} />
        </div>
        <div className="p-4 bg-[#0a0a0f] border-t border-gray-800">
          <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
            <input type="text" value={input} onChange={(e) => setInput(e.target.value)} onKeyPress={(e) => e.key === 'Enter' && sendMessage()} placeholder="Message MAX..." className="flex-1 bg-[#050508] border border-gray-700 rounded-xl px-4 py-3 text-white placeholder-gray-500 focus:border-purple-500 focus:outline-none" />
            <button onClick={sendMessage} disabled={isLoading || !input.trim()} className="w-12 h-12 rounded-xl bg-purple-600 hover:bg-purple-500 disabled:bg-gray-700 flex items-center justify-center text-white font-bold">→</button>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}


Save: Ctrl+O, Enter, Ctrl+X

Then restart:

bash
pkill -f "next dev"
sleep 2
cd ~/Empire/founder_dashboard && npm run dev > ~/Empire/logs/dashboard.log 2>&1 &
sleep 5
echo "Done! Refresh browser"


Now refresh your browser at http://localhost:3000

You should see:

✅ System Status panel in the sidebar showing Backend, Ollama, and Internet status
✅ Model selector defaulting to Claude
✅ Icons showing 🏠 for local (Ollama) and ☁️ for cloud (Claude)
✅ Yellow warning when internet is down and using Ollama fallback

Try sending "hello" in the dashboard - it should now show via claude ☁️ instead of via ollama-llama3.1 🏠.

Did it work? Let me know what you see!

🎉 It's working perfectly!

You now have:

✅ Claude Opus 4.5 as the default AI (see via claude-opus-4.5 ☁️)
✅ System Status panel showing all services
✅ Cloud icon (☁️) confirming it's using the internet API

The difference is clear:

Before	After
via ollama-llama3.1 🏠	via claude-opus-4.5 ☁️
Shorter responses	Detailed, professional responses

Quick test - try disconnecting your WiFi and send another message. It should:

Fall back to Ollama automatically
Show via ollama-llama3.1 🏠
Display the yellow "Using Ollama fallback" warning

What's next?

📱 Telegram notifications - Get alerts when internet goes down
🔧 MAX tools - Let MAX run empire-status via chat
📊 Desk agent tasks - Actually assign work to the 8 bots

Which one?

The /desks and /stats endpoints are missing! Let's check what routes exist and add the missing ones:

bash
grep -r "router\|@app" ~/Empire/backend/app --include="*.py" | grep -E "get|post" | head -20


And show the main router file:

bash
cat ~/Empire/backend/app/api/v1/max.py 2>/dev/null || cat ~/Empire/backend/app/routers/max.py 2>/dev/null || find ~/Empire/backend -name "*.py" | xargs grep -l "max" | head -5


Now let's see if there's a MAX router:

bash
cat ~/Empire/backend/app/routers/max.py 2>/dev/null || echo "No max.py found"
ls ~/Empire/backend/app/routers/


There's a max folder! Let's check what's in it:

bash
ls ~/Empire/backend/app/routers/max/
cat ~/Empire/backend/app/routers/max/__init__.py


Let's see the router:

bash
cat ~/Empire/backend/app/routers/max/router.py


The router exists and looks good. The issue is that it's mounted at /max but the frontend is calling /api/v1/max. Let's check how it's included in main.py:

bash
grep -A5 "include_router\|max" ~/Empire/backend/app/main.py


The router is being included! But there might be an import error. Let's check if the desk_manager is working:

bash
cd ~/Empire/backend && python -c "from app.services.max.desk_manager import desk_manager; print(desk_manager.get_all_desks())"


The desk_manager works! The backend just needs to be restarted. Let's restart it:

bash
# Kill existing backend
pkill -f "uvicorn"
sleep 2

# Start backend fresh
cd ~/Empire/backend && source venv/bin/activate && uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --reload > ~/Empire/logs/backend.log 2>&1 &
sleep 5

# Test the endpoints
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/desks | head -100
echo ""
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/stats


The uvicorn processes are owned by another user. Let's use sudo or find ours:

bash
# Find YOUR uvicorn process
ps aux | grep uvicorn | grep -v grep

# Kill just the ones you own
kill $(pgrep -u $USER uvicorn) 2>/dev/null

# Wait and test
sleep 3
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/desks | head -100


Good question! Let's find out:

bash
ps aux | grep uvicorn | grep -v grep


This will show the user in the first column. It might be:

root - if started with sudo
rg - your user (but from a different terminal/process)
A system service user

Let's check!

Wow! 12+ uvicorn processes running as root on port 8000! These were started at system boot (Feb21) - likely by a systemd service.

Let's check:

bash
# Check for systemd services
sudo systemctl list-units | grep -i empire

# Check what's using port 8000
sudo lsof -i :8000 | head -5


Enter your password for sudo, then we can see what's going on.

Or if you don't want to use sudo, just test if the current backend is working:

bash
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/desks | head -100


It seems the command output got cut off. Let's just test if the endpoints work:

bash
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/desks | head -100


Empty response - the endpoint isn't working. Let's check the backend logs and see what's actually running:

bash
# Check if backend is responding at all
curl -s http://localhost:8000/health
echo ""

# Check what routes are available
curl -s http://localhost:8000/docs 2>/dev/null | grep -o "/max[^\"]*" | head -10

# Check the backend log for errors
tail -50 ~/Empire/logs/backend.log 2>/dev/null | grep -i "error\|exception\|max"


Found the issue! There's an AttributeError - something is None when it shouldn't be. Let's see the full error:

bash
tail -100 ~/Empire/logs/backend.log | grep -A5 -B5 "AttributeError"


The licenses router is broken - it's returning None. Let's check and fix it:

bash
# Check the licenses router
cat ~/Empire/backend/app/routers/licenses.py | head -30


The licenses.py looks fine. The issue might be an import error in one of its dependencies. Let's test importing it directly:

bash
cd ~/Empire/backend && python -c "from app.routers import licenses; print(licenses.router)"


That works! The issue must be with import order in main.py. Let's check what's happening before the licenses import:

bash
# Test the full main.py import
cd ~/Empire/backend && python -c "from app.main import app; print('OK')" 2>&1 | head -30


It works when imported directly! The issue is the old root-owned processes are still holding port 8000. Let's kill the background process we started and use sudo to kill the old ones:

bash
# Kill our background process
kill 438377 2>/dev/null

# See if you have sudo access without password for specific commands
# Or just restart the old service properly
sudo systemctl restart empire-backend 2>/dev/null || echo "No systemd service"

# Or kill the root processes
sudo pkill -f "uvicorn.*8000"
sleep 2

# Now start fresh
cd ~/Empire/backend && source venv/bin/activate && uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --reload > ~/Empire/logs/backend.log 2>&1 &
sleep 5

# Test
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/desks | python -m json.tool | head -20


The process crashed. Let's check the log:

bash
cat ~/Empire/logs/backend.log


Port 8000 is still in use. The sudo pkill didn't work (probably needed password). Let's check and try again:

bash
# What's still on port 8000?
sudo lsof -i :8000 | head -5


Those are Firefox connections TO port 8000, not the server. Let's see the actual server:

bash
sudo lsof -i :8000 | grep LISTEN


Docker is running something on port 8000! Let's see what container:

bash
docker ps | grep 8000


There's the answer: empirebox-api-gateway is the Docker container serving port 8000!

Your backend is running in Docker, not directly. Let's check and restart that container:

bash
# Restart the API gateway to pick up changes
docker restart empirebox-api-gateway
sleep 5

# Test the MAX endpoints
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/desks | python -m json.tool | head -30


Good question! Let's check if the Docker container has your latest code or if it's outdated:

bash
# When was the container built vs when was your code last modified?
docker inspect empirebox-api-gateway --format='{{.Created}}'
echo "---"
ls -la ~/Empire/backend/app/routers/max/
echo "---"
ls -la ~/Empire/backend/app/services/max/


This will tell us if Docker has stale code.

The situation:

You have 14+ Docker containers running your Empire services
The api-gateway on port 8000 is likely an older build without your MAX code
Your local code has MAX but Docker doesn't know about it

Options:

Keep Docker - Rebuild the api-gateway image with your new MAX code
Ditch Docker - Stop the container and run uvicorn directly (simpler for dev)

Which do you prefer? For development, running directly is usually easier. For production, Docker is better.

Agreed! Let's stop the Docker container and run directly:

bash
# Stop the api-gateway container
docker stop empirebox-api-gateway
sleep 2

# Start your backend directly
cd ~/Empire/backend && source venv/bin/activate && uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --reload > ~/Empire/logs/backend.log 2>&1 &
sleep 5

# Test
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/desks | python -m json.tool | head -30


Let's check if it started properly:

bash
# Check the log
cat ~/Empire/logs/backend.log


Same error again. The import works standalone but fails in the subprocess. This is likely a circular import or environment issue. Let's check main.py imports:

bash
head -50 ~/Empire/backend/app/main.py


The issue is that it catches ImportError but not AttributeError. The licenses module imports but licenses.router is None. Let's check what's happening in the subprocess:

bash
# Test in a fresh Python process (simulating subprocess)
cd ~/Empire/backend && python -c "
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '.')
from app.routers import licenses
print('licenses module:', licenses)
print('licenses.router:', licenses.router)
print('router type:', type(licenses.router))
"


Works fine in isolation. The issue might be in the routers __init__.py. Let's check:

bash
cat ~/Empire/backend/app/routers/__init__.py


The __init__.py catches ImportError but not other exceptions. If licenses.py raises any other exception during import, it silently sets licenses = None.

Let's test importing licenses directly to see if there's a hidden error:

bash
cd ~/Empire/backend && python -c "
import traceback
try:
    from app.routers.licenses import router
    print('SUCCESS:', router)
except Exception as e:
    traceback.print_exc()
"


Still works. The issue must be with the reload mechanism in uvicorn. Let's try running without --reload:

bash
# Kill the old process
pkill -f "uvicorn.*8000"
sleep 2

# Start without --reload
cd ~/Empire/backend && source venv/bin/activate && uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 > ~/Empire/logs/backend.log 2>&1 &
sleep 5

# Test
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/desks | python -m json.tool | head -30


Those root-owned uvicorn processes from Docker are blocking. Let's use sudo to kill them:

bash
sudo pkill -f "uvicorn.*8000"
sleep 2

# Now start fresh
cd ~/Empire/backend && uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 > ~/Empire/logs/backend.log 2>&1 &
sleep 5

# Test
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/desks | python -m json.tool | head -30


It's working! 🎉 The MAX endpoints are live now.

To prevent Docker from grabbing port 8000 again on reboot, let's disable auto-restart for that container:

bash
# Prevent api-gateway from auto-starting
docker update --restart=no empirebox-api-gateway

# Verify MAX is fully functional
echo "=== MAX Health ==="
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/health | python -m json.tool

echo ""
echo "=== All Desks ==="
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/desks | python -m json.tool


MAX is fully operational! 🚀

Summary:

✅ 8 desks online (DevBot, OpsBot, SalesBot, SupportBot, FinanceBot, ContentBot, ProductBot, QABot)
✅ Health endpoint working
✅ Running directly on port 8000 (no Docker dependency)
✅ Docker api-gateway won't auto-restart

One thing to note: telegram_configured: false - you'll need to set TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN in your environment when you're ready for Telegram integration.

Now refresh Firefox and your MAX dashboard should be working! Want to test sending a task to one of the desks?

1. Telegram Setup

To set up Telegram:

Open Telegram and message @BotFather

Give it a name like "MAX Empire Assistant"
You'll get a token like 123456789:ABCdefGHIjklMNOpqrsTUVwxyz

Once you have the token, we'll add it to your environment.

2. Save This Chat

This chat is on GitHub Copilot - it's not automatically saved. To preserve it:

Copy/paste the full conversation to a file, or

3. USB Backup Approach

Your instinct is good but needs refinement:

A bootable USB with EmpireBox is useful for disaster recovery, but for a master backup, you want:

What	Best Approach
Code	Git repo (you have this)
Database	pg_dump to encrypted file
Config/Secrets	Encrypted .env backup
Full System Image	dd or Clonezilla of the drive

Recommended:

Code
USB Structure:
├── empire-backup-YYYY-MM-DD/
│   ├── database.sql.gpg      # Encrypted DB dump
│   ├── env-secrets.gpg       # Encrypted configs
│   ├── code/                  # Git clone of Empire
│   └── RECOVERY.md           # Instructions


Want me to create a backup script that does this properly?

Based on your setup, try:

bash
# Check what's running on common frontend ports
echo "Frontend ports:"
sudo lsof -i :3000 -i :5173 -i :8080 | grep LISTEN

echo ""
echo "Docker frontends:"
docker ps | grep -E "frontend|dashboard|web|ui"


Most likely URLs:

http://localhost:3000 - If React/Next.js frontend
http://localhost:5173 - If Vite dev server
http://localhost:8080 - Common alternative

Or if you're accessing remotely:

http://EmpireBox:3000 (or your machine's IP)

What does your browser currently show when you access the Empire dashboard?

The dashboard is working! The "Claude shows as no key" just means you haven't configured a Claude API key - but you're using Ollama locally which is better (no external dependency).

Let's verify your AI setup:

bash
# Check your AI config
grep -E "CLAUDE|OLLAMA|OPENAI|AI_PROVIDER" ~/Empire/backend/.env 2>/dev/null

echo ""
echo "=== Ollama status ==="
curl -s http://localhost:11434/api/tags | python -m json.tool | head -20


Your current setup:

✅ Backend: 200 (working)
✅ Ollama: 200 (local AI - no API key needed!)
✅ Internet: Online
✅ All 8 AI Desks online
✅ MAX responding

You don't need a Claude key since Ollama is your primary AI. The dashboard is just showing it's not configured, which is fine.

Now for Telegram - do you have a bot token from @BotFather, or should I walk you through creating one?

Understood. You want Claude Opus as primary, Ollama as fallback.

Let's set that up:

bash
# Check current AI config
cat ~/Empire/backend/.env | grep -E "CLAUDE|ANTHROPIC|OLLAMA|AI_|LLM_|MODEL"


Then I need your Anthropic API key to configure Claude Opus.

Do you have one? If not:

Go to https://console.anthropic.com/
Create account or login
Go to API Keys → Create Key
Copy the key (starts with sk-ant-...)

Once you have it, we'll configure:

Code
AI_PRIMARY_PROVIDER=anthropic
AI_PRIMARY_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-20250514
AI_FALLBACK_PROVIDER=ollama
AI_FALLBACK_MODEL=llama3
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-xxxxx


Note: Claude Opus 4.5 isn't released yet - the current best is Claude Sonnet 4 or Claude 3.5 Sonnet. Which do you want as primary?

Good, you have the Anthropic key! Now let's add the AI configuration:

bash
# Add AI provider config to .env
cat >> ~/Empire/backend/.env << 'EOF'

# AI Configuration - Claude Primary, Ollama Fallback
AI_PRIMARY_PROVIDER=anthropic
AI_PRIMARY_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-20250514
AI_FALLBACK_PROVIDER=ollama
AI_FALLBACK_MODEL=llama3
OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434
EOF

# Verify
echo "=== Updated AI Config ==="
grep -E "CLAUDE|ANTHROPIC|OLLAMA|AI_|MODEL" ~/Empire/backend/.env

# Restart backend to pick up changes
pkill -f "uvicorn.*8000"
sleep 2
cd ~/Empire/backend && uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 > ~/Empire/logs/backend.log 2>&1 &
sleep 5

# Test MAX health
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/health | python -m json.tool


This sets:

Primary: Claude Sonnet 4 (latest/best available)
Fallback: Ollama/Llama3 (local, if Claude fails)

After this, we'll set up Telegram. Do you have a bot token from @BotFather?

sleep 3

sleep 5

# Check if running
cat ~/Empire/logs/backend.log | tail -10
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/health | python -m json.tool

Wait, that's the old content. Let me write and verify the update:

bash
# Write updated config
cat > ~/Empire/backend/app/core/config.py << 'EOF'
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
from typing import Optional


class Settings(BaseSettings):
    DATABASE_URL: str = "postgresql://user:password@localhost:5432/marketf"
    SECRET_KEY: str = "your-secret-key-change-in-production"
    ALGORITHM: str = "HS256"
    ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES: int = 30
    STRIPE_SECRET_KEY: str = ""
    STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY: str = ""
    MARKETF_FEE_PERCENT: float = 8.0
    MARKETF_ESCROW_RELEASE_DELAY_HOURS: int = 48
    PAYMENT_PROCESSING_FEE_PERCENT: float = 2.9
    PAYMENT_PROCESSING_FEE_FIXED: float = 0.30
    
    # API Keys
    anthropic_api_key: Optional[str] = None
    xai_api_key: Optional[str] = None
    openai_api_key: Optional[str] = None
    
    # AI Configuration
    ai_primary_provider: str = "anthropic"
    ai_primary_model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
    ai_fallback_provider: str = "ollama"
    ai_fallback_model: str = "llama3"
    ollama_base_url: str = "http://localhost:11434"
    
    # Telegram
    telegram_bot_token: Optional[str] = None
    
    class Config:
        env_file = ".env"
        extra = "ignore"


settings = Settings()
EOF

# Verify it was written
cat ~/Empire/backend/app/core/config.py

# Restart backend

There are TWO venvs! It's using /home/rg/Empire/venv but we're in /home/rg/Empire/backend/venv. And maybe there's a cached version. Let's check:

bash
# Check if there's another config.py being used
find ~/Empire -name "config.py" -path "*/core/*" 2>/dev/null

# Check which python is running
which python
echo "---"
ls -la ~/Empire/venv 2>/dev/null

Found it! You're using ~/Empire/venv but the backend is at ~/Empire/backend. Let's use the correct venv:

bash
# Deactivate current and use the root Empire venv
deactivate 2>/dev/null
source ~/Empire/venv/bin/activate

# Clear Python cache
find ~/Empire -type d -name "__pycache__" -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null

# Verify config is updated
cat ~/Empire/backend/app/core/config.py | grep -A5 "AI Configuration"

# Run from correct location
cd ~/Empire/backend && python -c "from app.core.config import settings; print('Provider:', settings.ai_primary_provider)"

cd ~/Empire/backend && uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 > ~/Empire/logs/backend.log 2>&1 &
sleep 5

# Test
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/health | python -m json.tool


Let's check the error:

bash
cat ~/Empire/logs/backend.log


There are TWO config files! One at app/core/config.py and another at app/config.py. Let's update the second one:

bash
# Check the other config file

Found it! This is the main config being used. Let's add the AI provider settings:

bash
cat > ~/Empire/backend/app/config.py << 'EOF'
"""
Configuration settings for MarketForge backend.
Loads environment variables and provides centralized config.
"""
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
from typing import Optional


class Settings(BaseSettings):
    """Application settings loaded from environment variables."""
    
    # Database
    database_url: str = "postgresql+asyncpg://user:pass@localhost:5432/marketforge"
    
    # Security
    secret_key: str = "your-secret-key-here-change-in-production"
    algorithm: str = "HS256"
    access_token_expire_minutes: int = 30
    refresh_token_expire_days: int = 7
    
    # eBay API
    ebay_app_id: Optional[str] = None
    ebay_cert_id: Optional[str] = None
    ebay_dev_id: Optional[str] = None
    
    # Stripe
    stripe_secret_key: Optional[str] = None
    stripe_webhook_secret: Optional[str] = None
    
    # Cloudflare Email
    cloudflare_api_key: Optional[str] = None
    cloudflare_zone_id: Optional[str] = None
    
    # AI Services
    grok_api_key: Optional[str] = None
    anthropic_api_key: Optional[str] = None
    xai_api_key: Optional[str] = None
    openai_api_key: Optional[str] = None
    ollama_base_url: str = "http://localhost:11434"
    
    # AI Provider Configuration (Claude Primary, Ollama Fallback)
    ai_primary_provider: str = "anthropic"
    ai_primary_model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
    ai_fallback_provider: str = "ollama"
    ai_fallback_model: str = "llama3"
    
    # Telegram
    telegram_bot_token: Optional[str] = None
    
    # Economic Intelligence
    economic_enabled: bool = True
    economic_default_balance: float = 1000.00
    economic_token_input_price_per_1m: float = 2.50
    economic_token_output_price_per_1m: float = 10.00
    economic_compute_cost_per_minute: float = 0.10
    economic_listing_commission_rate: float = 0.05
    
    # Quality Evaluation
    quality_eval_enabled: bool = True
    quality_eval_model: str = "gpt-4-turbo-preview"
    
    # Chat Backup and Decision Context
    chat_backup_enabled: bool = True
    chat_backup_interval_hours: int = 6
    chat_backup_dir: str = "/tmp/chat_backups"
    chat_backup_cloud_enabled: bool = False
    chat_backup_cloud_bucket: Optional[str] = None
    chat_backup_retention_days: int = 90
    
    # CORS
    cors_origins: list = ["http://localhost:3000", "http://localhost:8080"]
    
    class Config:
        env_file = ".env"
        case_sensitive = False


# Global settings instance
settings = Settings()
EOF

# Clear cache again
find ~/Empire -type d -name "__pycache__" -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null

# Start backend
cd ~/Empire/backend && uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 > ~/Empire/logs/backend.log 2>&1 &
sleep 5

curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/health | python -m json.tool

# Find the frontend source
find ~/Empire -type d -name "src" 2>/dev/null | head -5
find ~/Empire -name "*.tsx" 2>/dev/null | head -5


Found it! The frontend is at ~/Empire/founder_dashboard. Let's find the Claude/Opus check:

bash
grep -r "opus\|claude\|anthropic\|no key" ~/Empire/founder_dashboard/src --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" -i | head -20

grep -A 30 "models" ~/Empire/founder_dashboard/src/app/page.tsx | head -40


The frontend fetches models from /max/models. Let's check what that endpoint returns:

bash
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/models | python -m json.tool


The backend still shows Claude as unavailable. Let's find and fix the MAX router:

bash
grep -r "Claude Opus\|available.*false\|models" ~/Empire/backend/app/routers/max*.py | head -20


bash
find ~/Empire/backend -name "*.py" -exec grep -l "Claude Opus\|max/models" {} \;


bash
cat ~/Empire/backend/app/services/max/ai_router.py


Found it! The router checks os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY") but your .env has anthropic_api_key (lowercase). Let's check and fix:

bash
# Check the .env file
grep -i anthropic ~/Empire/backend/.env

# Also check what's set in environment
echo "ENV VAR: $ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"


The key is in .env but not loaded into the environment. The backend needs to export it. Let's restart with the env loaded:

bash
# Kill existing backend
pkill -f "uvicorn app.main"

# Export from .env and restart
export $(grep -v '^#' ~/Empire/backend/.env | xargs)
echo "Loaded: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:0:20}..."

# Restart backend
cd ~/Empire/backend && uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 > ~/Empire/logs/backend.log 2>&1 &
sleep 5

# Test
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/max/models | python -m json.tool

# Start frontend

npm run dev > ~/Empire/logs/frontend.log 2>&1 &

echo "EmpireBox started!"

echo "Backend: http://localhost:8000"

Here's the full file with the textarea fix:

bash
cat > ~/Empire/founder_dashboard/src/app/page.tsx << 'EOF'
'use client';

import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from 'react';

const API_URL = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://localhost:8000/api/v1';

interface Message {
  role: 'user' | 'assistant';
  content: string;
  model?: string;
}

interface Desk {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  status: string;
  stats: { completed: number; failed: number };
}

interface AIModel {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  available: boolean;
  type: string;
}

interface SystemStatus {
  backend: string;
  ollama: string;
  internet: boolean;
}

export default function FounderDashboard() {
  const [messages, setMessages] = useState<Message[]>([
    { role: 'assistant', content: "Hello! I'm MAX, your AI Assistant Manager. All 8 desks are online. How can I help?" }
  ]);
  const [input, setInput] = useState('');
  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false);
  const [desks, setDesks] = useState<Desk[]>([]);
  const [models, setModels] = useState<AIModel[]>([]);
  const [selectedModel, setSelectedModel] = useState('claude');
  const [stats, setStats] = useState({ total_completed: 0, active_tasks: 0 });
  const [systemStatus, setSystemStatus] = useState<SystemStatus>({ backend: '...', ollama: '...', internet: true });
  const messagesEndRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchDesks();
    fetchModels();
    fetchStats();
    checkSystemStatus();
    const interval = setInterval(() => { fetchDesks(); fetchStats(); checkSystemStatus(); }, 30000);
    return () => clearInterval(interval);
  }, []);

  useEffect(() => {
    messagesEndRef.current?.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth' });
  }, [messages]);

  const fetchDesks = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(API_URL + '/max/desks');
      const data = await res.json();
      setDesks(data.desks || []);
    } catch (e) { console.error('Failed to fetch desks:', e); }
  };

  const fetchModels = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(API_URL + '/max/models');
      const data = await res.json();
      setModels(data.models || []);
    } catch (e) { console.error('Failed to fetch models:', e); }
  };

  const fetchStats = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(API_URL + '/max/stats');
      const data = await res.json();
      setStats(data.stats || {});
    } catch (e) { console.error('Failed to fetch stats:', e); }
  };

  const checkSystemStatus = async () => {
    try {
      const backendRes = await fetch(API_URL.replace('/api/v1', '') + '/health');
      const backendOk = backendRes.ok ? '200' : 'ERR';
      let ollamaOk = 'OFF';
      try {
        const ollamaRes = await fetch('http://localhost:11434/api/tags');
        ollamaOk = ollamaRes.ok ? '200' : 'ERR';
      } catch { ollamaOk = 'OFF'; }
      setSystemStatus({ backend: backendOk, ollama: ollamaOk, internet: true });
    } catch (e) {
      setSystemStatus({ backend: 'ERR', ollama: 'ERR', internet: false });
    }
  };

  const sendMessage = async () => {
    if (!input.trim() || isLoading) return;
    const userMessage: Message = { role: 'user', content: input };
    setMessages(prev => [...prev, userMessage]);
    setInput('');
    setIsLoading(true);
    try {
      const res = await fetch(API_URL + '/max/chat', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ message: input, model: selectedModel, history: messages.map(m => ({ role: m.role, content: m.content })) })
      });
      const data = await res.json();
      const modelUsed = data.model_used || selectedModel;
      const isOffline = modelUsed.includes('ollama');
      if (isOffline && systemStatus.internet) {
        setSystemStatus(prev => ({ ...prev, internet: false }));
      }
      setMessages(prev => [...prev, { role: 'assistant', content: data.response || 'Error processing request.', model: modelUsed }]);
    } catch (e) {
      setMessages(prev => [...prev, { role: 'assistant', content: 'Connection error. Check if backend is running.' }]);
    } finally { setIsLoading(false); }
  };

  const getStatusColor = (status: string) => status === 'idle' ? 'bg-green-500' : status === 'busy' ? 'bg-yellow-500' : 'bg-gray-500';
  const getStatusDot = (status: string) => status === '200' ? 'bg-green-500' : status === 'OFF' ? 'bg-yellow-500' : 'bg-red-500';

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen bg-[#050508] flex">
      <div className="w-72 bg-[#0a0a0f] border-r border-gray-800 flex flex-col">
        <div className="p-4 border-b border-gray-800">
          <h1 className="text-xl font-bold text-purple-400">EmpireBox</h1>
          <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">Founder Edition</p>
        </div>
        <div className="p-3 border-b border-gray-800">
          <h3 className="text-xs font-semibold text-gray-500 uppercase mb-2">System Status</h3>
          <div className="space-y-1 text-xs">
            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
              <span className="text-gray-400">Backend</span>
              <div className="flex items-center gap-1"><div className={`w-2 h-2 rounded-full ${getStatusDot(systemStatus.backend)}`} /><span className="text-gray-500">{systemStatus.backend}</span></div>
            </div>
            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
              <span className="text-gray-400">Ollama</span>
              <div className="flex items-center gap-1"><div className={`w-2 h-2 rounded-full ${getStatusDot(systemStatus.ollama)}`} /><span className="text-gray-500">{systemStatus.ollama}</span></div>
            </div>
            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
              <span className="text-gray-400">Internet</span>
              <div className="flex items-center gap-1"><div className={`w-2 h-2 rounded-full ${systemStatus.internet ? 'bg-green-500' : 'bg-red-500'}`} /><span className="text-gray-500">{systemStatus.internet ? 'Online' : 'Offline'}</span></div>
            </div>
          </div>
          {!systemStatus.internet && <div className="mt-2 p-2 bg-yellow-900/30 border border-yellow-700 rounded text-xs text-yellow-400">Using Ollama fallback</div>}
        </div>
        <div className="p-3 grid grid-cols-2 gap-2">
          <div className="bg-[#050508] rounded-lg p-3 border border-gray-800"><span className="text-xs text-gray-400">Completed</span><p className="text-xl font-bold text-white">{stats.total_completed}</p></div>
          <div className="bg-[#050508] rounded-lg p-3 border border-gray-800"><span className="text-xs text-gray-400">Active</span><p className="text-xl font-bold text-white">{stats.active_tasks}</p></div>
        </div>
        <div className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-3">
          <h3 className="text-xs font-semibold text-gray-500 uppercase mb-2 px-2">AI Desks</h3>
          {desks.map(desk => (
            <div key={desk.id} className="flex items-center gap-3 p-2 rounded-lg hover:bg-[#050508] cursor-pointer">
              <div className="relative">
                <div className="w-8 h-8 rounded-lg bg-gray-800 flex items-center justify-center text-gray-400 text-sm">{desk.name.charAt(0)}</div>
                <div className={`absolute -bottom-0.5 -right-0.5 w-2.5 h-2.5 rounded-full border-2 border-[#0a0a0f] ${getStatusColor(desk.status)}`} />
              </div>
              <div className="flex-1"><p className="text-sm font-medium text-gray-200">{desk.name}</p><p className="text-xs text-gray-500">{desk.description}</p></div>
            </div>
          ))}
        </div>
      </div>
      <div className="flex-1 flex flex-col">
        <div className="h-14 bg-[#0a0a0f] border-b border-gray-800 flex items-center justify-between px-4">
          <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
            <div className="w-8 h-8 rounded-full bg-purple-600 flex items-center justify-center text-white font-bold">M</div>
            <div><h2 className="font-semibold text-white">MAX</h2><p className="text-xs text-gray-400">AI Assistant Manager</p></div>
          </div>
          <select value={selectedModel} onChange={(e) => setSelectedModel(e.target.value)} className="bg-[#050508] border border-gray-700 rounded-lg px-3 py-1.5 text-sm text-gray-300">
            {models.map(m => (<option key={m.id} value={m.id} disabled={!m.available}>{m.name} {!m.available && '(No Key)'}</option>))}
          </select>
        </div>
        <div className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-4 space-y-4">
          {messages.map((msg, i) => (
            <div key={i} className={`flex ${msg.role === 'user' ? 'justify-end' : 'justify-start'}`}>
              <div className={`max-w-[80%] rounded-2xl px-4 py-3 ${msg.role === 'user' ? 'bg-purple-600 text-white' : 'bg-[#0a0a0f] border border-gray-800 text-gray-200'}`}>
                <p className="whitespace-pre-wrap">{msg.content}</p>
                {msg.model && <p className="text-xs mt-2 opacity-50">via {msg.model} {msg.model.includes('ollama') ? '🏠' : '☁️'}</p>}
              </div>
            </div>
          ))}
          {isLoading && <div className="flex justify-start"><div className="bg-[#0a0a0f] border border-gray-800 rounded-2xl px-4 py-3 text-gray-400">Thinking...</div></div>}
          <div ref={messagesEndRef} />
        </div>
        <div className="p-4 bg-[#0a0a0f] border-t border-gray-800">
          <div className="flex items-start gap-3">
            <textarea 
              value={input} 
              onChange={(e) => setInput(e.target.value)} 
              onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) { e.preventDefault(); sendMessage(); }}} 
              placeholder="Message MAX... (Shift+Enter for new line)" 
              rows={3} 
              className="flex-1 bg-[#050508] border border-gray-700 rounded-xl px-4 py-3 text-white placeholder-gray-500 focus:border-purple-500 focus:outline-none resize-y min-h-[48px] max-h-[200px]" 
            />
            <button onClick={sendMessage} disabled={isLoading || !input.trim()} className="w-12 h-12 rounded-xl bg-purple-600 hover:bg-purple-500 disabled:bg-gray-700 flex items-center justify-center text-white font-bold">→</button>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
EOF
echo "✅ Updated! Refresh your browser."

import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from 'react';

const API_URL = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://localhost:8000/api/v1';

Quick questions

So let me build the file upload feature. It's a code task - no need to burn Claude tokens.

What type of files do you want to upload?

Documents for MAX to read (PDFs, text, etc.)
Code files to add to the project
Images/assets

And where should uploads go - just to MAX for analysis, or stored in the system for desk agents too?

Smart architecture. Let me build it:

File Upload System:

Upload → stored in ~/Empire/uploads/
MAX indexes all files
Desk agents request via MAX (logged)
Full audit trail
bash
# Create upload directory structure
mkdir -p ~/Empire/uploads/{documents,code,images,other}
mkdir -p ~/Empire/logs/file_access

# Create the file upload backend endpoint
cat > ~/Empire/backend/app/api/v1/files.py << 'EOF'
"""File upload and access system with audit logging."""
import os
import json
import shutil
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
from fastapi import APIRouter, UploadFile, File, HTTPException
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import Optional, List

router = APIRouter(prefix="/files", tags=["files"])

UPLOAD_DIR = Path.home() / "Empire" / "uploads"
LOG_DIR = Path.home() / "Empire" / "logs" / "file_access"

# Ensure directories exist
UPLOAD_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
LOG_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

def get_category(filename: str) -> str:
    ext = filename.lower().split('.')[-1] if '.' in filename else ''
    if ext in ['pdf', 'txt', 'md', 'doc', 'docx', 'csv', 'json']:
        return 'documents'
    elif ext in ['py', 'js', 'ts', 'tsx', 'jsx', 'html', 'css', 'sh', 'yaml', 'yml']:
        return 'code'
    elif ext in ['png', 'jpg', 'jpeg', 'gif', 'svg', 'webp', 'ico']:
        return 'images'
    return 'other'

def log_access(action: str, filename: str, agent: str, details: str = ""):
    log_entry = {
        "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
        "action": action,
        "filename": filename,
        "agent": agent,
        "details": details
    }
    log_file = LOG_DIR / f"{datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}.jsonl"
    with open(log_file, 'a') as f:
        f.write(json.dumps(log_entry) + '\n')
    return log_entry

class FileInfo(BaseModel):
    name: str
    category: str
    size: int
    uploaded_at: str
    path: str

class FileRequest(BaseModel):
    filename: str
    requesting_agent: str
    reason: Optional[str] = ""

class FileListResponse(BaseModel):
    files: List[FileInfo]
    total: int

@router.post("/upload")
async def upload_file(file: UploadFile = File(...), uploader: str = "founder"):
    """Upload a file - founder only."""
    if not file.filename:
        raise HTTPException(400, "No filename provided")
    
    category = get_category(file.filename)
    save_path = UPLOAD_DIR / category / file.filename
    
    # Don't overwrite - add timestamp if exists
    if save_path.exists():
        stem = save_path.stem
        suffix = save_path.suffix
        timestamp = datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')
        save_path = UPLOAD_DIR / category / f"{stem}_{timestamp}{suffix}"
    
    with open(save_path, 'wb') as f:
        shutil.copyfileobj(file.file, f)
    
    log_access("upload", file.filename, uploader, f"category={category}")
    
    return {
        "status": "success",
        "filename": save_path.name,
        "category": category,
        "path": str(save_path),
        "size": save_path.stat().st_size
    }

@router.get("/list")
async def list_files(category: Optional[str] = None) -> FileListResponse:
    """List all uploaded files."""
    files = []
    categories = [category] if category else ['documents', 'code', 'images', 'other']
    
    for cat in categories:
        cat_path = UPLOAD_DIR / cat
        if cat_path.exists():
            for f in cat_path.iterdir():
                if f.is_file():
                    stat = f.stat()
                    files.append(FileInfo(
                        name=f.name,
                        category=cat,
                        size=stat.st_size,
                        uploaded_at=datetime.fromtimestamp(stat.st_mtime).isoformat(),
                        path=str(f)
                    ))
    
    return FileListResponse(files=files, total=len(files))

@router.post("/request")
async def request_file(req: FileRequest):
    """Desk agent requests file access - logged."""
    # Find the file
    for cat in ['documents', 'code', 'images', 'other']:
        file_path = UPLOAD_DIR / cat / req.filename
        if file_path.exists():
            log_access("request", req.filename, req.requesting_agent, req.reason)
            
            # Read content for text files
            content = None
            if cat in ['documents', 'code'] and file_path.stat().st_size < 100000:
                try:
                    content = file_path.read_text()
                except:
                    content = f"[Binary file: {file_path.stat().st_size} bytes]"
            
            return {
                "status": "granted",
                "filename": req.filename,
                "category": cat,
                "path": str(file_path),
                "content": content,
                "logged": True
            }
    
    log_access("request_denied", req.filename, req.requesting_agent, "file not found")
    raise HTTPException(404, f"File not found: {req.filename}")

@router.get("/logs")
async def get_access_logs(date: Optional[str] = None, agent: Optional[str] = None):
    """Get file access audit logs."""
    target_date = date or datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
    log_file = LOG_DIR / f"{target_date}.jsonl"
    
    if not log_file.exists():
        return {"logs": [], "date": target_date}
    
    logs = []
    with open(log_file, 'r') as f:
        for line in f:
            entry = json.loads(line.strip())
            if agent is None or entry.get('agent') == agent:
                logs.append(entry)
    
    return {"logs": logs, "date": target_date, "total": len(logs)}
EOF

echo "✅ File API created"


Now register it in the main app:

bash
# Add files router to main.py
sed -i '/from app.api.v1 import max_routes/a from app.api.v1 import files' ~/Empire/backend/app/main.py
sed -i '/app.include_router(max_routes.router/a app.include_router(files.router, prefix="/api/v1")' ~/Empire/backend/app/main.py

# Restart backend
pkill -f "uvicorn app.main"
cd ~/Empire/backend && source ~/Empire/venv/bin/activate
export $(grep -v '^#' ~/Empire/backend/.env | xargs)
uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 > ~/Empire/logs/backend.log 2>&1 &
sleep 3

# Test
curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/v1/files/list | python -m json.tool


Run those commands and I'll add the upload UI to the dashboard next.

Got it - you want the input textarea to auto-expand as you type, up to max 50% of the chat area. Let me update the dashboard:

bash
cat > ~/Empire/founder_dashboard/src/app/page.tsx << 'EOF'
'use client';

import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from 'react';

const API_URL = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://localhost:8000/api/v1';

interface Message {
  role: 'user' | 'assistant';
  content: string;
  model?: string;
}

interface Desk {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  status: string;
  stats: { completed: number; failed: number };
}

interface AIModel {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  available: boolean;
  type: string;
}

interface SystemStatus {
  backend: string;
  ollama: string;
  internet: boolean;
}

export default function FounderDashboard() {
  const [messages, setMessages] = useState<Message[]>([
    { role: 'assistant', content: "Hello! I'm MAX, your AI Assistant Manager. All 8 desks are online. How can I help?" }
  ]);
  const [input, setInput] = useState('');
  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false);
  const [desks, setDesks] = useState<Desk[]>([]);
  const [models, setModels] = useState<AIModel[]>([]);
  const [selectedModel, setSelectedModel] = useState('claude');
  const [stats, setStats] = useState({ total_completed: 0, active_tasks: 0 });
  const [systemStatus, setSystemStatus] = useState<SystemStatus>({ backend: '...', ollama: '...', internet: true });
  const messagesEndRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
  const textareaRef = useRef<HTMLTextAreaElement>(null);
  const chatContainerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchDesks();
    fetchModels();
    fetchStats();
    checkSystemStatus();
    const interval = setInterval(() => { fetchDesks(); fetchStats(); checkSystemStatus(); }, 30000);
    return () => clearInterval(interval);
  }, []);

  useEffect(() => {
    messagesEndRef.current?.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth' });
  }, [messages]);

  // Auto-resize textarea
  useEffect(() => {
    if (textareaRef.current && chatContainerRef.current) {
      const textarea = textareaRef.current;
      const chatContainer = chatContainerRef.current;
      
      // Reset height to measure properly
      textarea.style.height = 'auto';
      
      // Calculate max height (50% of chat container)
      const maxHeight = chatContainer.clientHeight * 0.5;
      const minHeight = 48;
      
      // Set new height
      const newHeight = Math.min(Math.max(textarea.scrollHeight, minHeight), maxHeight);
      textarea.style.height = `${newHeight}px`;
    }
  }, [input]);

  const fetchDesks = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(API_URL + '/max/desks');
      const data = await res.json();
      setDesks(data.desks || []);
    } catch (e) { console.error('Failed to fetch desks:', e); }
  };

  const fetchModels = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(API_URL + '/max/models');
      const data = await res.json();
      setModels(data.models || []);
    } catch (e) { console.error('Failed to fetch models:', e); }
  };

  const fetchStats = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(API_URL + '/max/stats');
      const data = await res.json();
      setStats(data.stats || {});
    } catch (e) { console.error('Failed to fetch stats:', e); }
  };

  const checkSystemStatus = async () => {
    try {
      const backendRes = await fetch(API_URL.replace('/api/v1', '') + '/health');
      const backendOk = backendRes.ok ? '200' : 'ERR';
      let ollamaOk = 'OFF';
      try {
        const ollamaRes = await fetch('http://localhost:11434/api/tags');
        ollamaOk = ollamaRes.ok ? '200' : 'ERR';
      } catch { ollamaOk = 'OFF'; }
      setSystemStatus({ backend: backendOk, ollama: ollamaOk, internet: true });
    } catch (e) {
      setSystemStatus({ backend: 'ERR', ollama: 'ERR', internet: false });
    }
  };

  const sendMessage = async () => {
    if (!input.trim() || isLoading) return;
    const userMessage: Message = { role: 'user', content: input };
    setMessages(prev => [...prev, userMessage]);
    setInput('');
    setIsLoading(true);
    try {
      const res = await fetch(API_URL + '/max/chat', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ message: input, model: selectedModel, history: messages.map(m => ({ role: m.role, content: m.content })) })
      });
      const data = await res.json();
      const modelUsed = data.model_used || selectedModel;
      const isOffline = modelUsed.includes('ollama');
      if (isOffline && systemStatus.internet) {
        setSystemStatus(prev => ({ ...prev, internet: false }));
      }
      setMessages(prev => [...prev, { role: 'assistant', content: data.response || 'Error processing request.', model: modelUsed }]);
    } catch (e) {
      setMessages(prev => [...prev, { role: 'assistant', content: 'Connection error. Check if backend is running.' }]);
    } finally { setIsLoading(false); }
  };

  const getStatusColor = (status: string) => status === 'idle' ? 'bg-green-500' : status === 'busy' ? 'bg-yellow-500' : 'bg-gray-500';
  const getStatusDot = (status: string) => status === '200' ? 'bg-green-500' : status === 'OFF' ? 'bg-yellow-500' : 'bg-red-500';

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen bg-[#050508] flex">
      <div className="w-72 bg-[#0a0a0f] border-r border-gray-800 flex flex-col">
        <div className="p-4 border-b border-gray-800">
          <h1 className="text-xl font-bold text-purple-400">EmpireBox</h1>
          <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">Founder Edition</p>
        </div>
        <div className="p-3 border-b border-gray-800">
          <h3 className="text-xs font-semibold text-gray-500 uppercase mb-2">System Status</h3>
          <div className="space-y-1 text-xs">
            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
              <span className="text-gray-400">Backend</span>
              <div className="flex items-center gap-1"><div className={`w-2 h-2 rounded-full ${getStatusDot(systemStatus.backend)}`} /><span className="text-gray-500">{systemStatus.backend}</span></div>
            </div>
            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
              <span className="text-gray-400">Ollama</span>
              <div className="flex items-center gap-1"><div className={`w-2 h-2 rounded-full ${getStatusDot(systemStatus.ollama)}`} /><span className="text-gray-500">{systemStatus.ollama}</span></div>
            </div>
            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
              <span className="text-gray-400">Internet</span>
              <div className="flex items-center gap-1"><div className={`w-2 h-2 rounded-full ${systemStatus.internet ? 'bg-green-500' : 'bg-red-500'}`} /><span className="text-gray-500">{systemStatus.internet ? 'Online' : 'Offline'}</span></div>
            </div>
          </div>
          {!systemStatus.internet && <div className="mt-2 p-2 bg-yellow-900/30 border border-yellow-700 rounded text-xs text-yellow-400">Using Ollama fallback</div>}
        </div>
        <div className="p-3 grid grid-cols-2 gap-2">
          <div className="bg-[#050508] rounded-lg p-3 border border-gray-800"><span className="text-xs text-gray-400">Completed</span><p className="text-xl font-bold text-white">{stats.total_completed}</p></div>
          <div className="bg-[#050508] rounded-lg p-3 border border-gray-800"><span className="text-xs text-gray-400">Active</span><p className="text-xl font-bold text-white">{stats.active_tasks}</p></div>
        </div>
        <div className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-3">
          <h3 className="text-xs font-semibold text-gray-500 uppercase mb-2 px-2">AI Desks</h3>
          {desks.map(desk => (
            <div key={desk.id} className="flex items-center gap-3 p-2 rounded-lg hover:bg-[#050508] cursor-pointer">
              <div className="relative">
                <div className="w-8 h-8 rounded-lg bg-gray-800 flex items-center justify-center text-gray-400 text-sm">{desk.name.charAt(0)}</div>
                <div className={`absolute -bottom-0.5 -right-0.5 w-2.5 h-2.5 rounded-full border-2 border-[#0a0a0f] ${getStatusColor(desk.status)}`} />
              </div>
              <div className="flex-1"><p className="text-sm font-medium text-gray-200">{desk.name}</p><p className="text-xs text-gray-500">{desk.description}</p></div>
            </div>
          ))}
        </div>
      </div>
      <div className="flex-1 flex flex-col">
        <div className="h-14 bg-[#0a0a0f] border-b border-gray-800 flex items-center justify-between px-4">
          <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
            <div className="w-8 h-8 rounded-full bg-purple-600 flex items-center justify-center text-white font-bold">M</div>
            <div><h2 className="font-semibold text-white">MAX</h2><p className="text-xs text-gray-400">AI Assistant Manager</p></div>
          </div>
          <select value={selectedModel} onChange={(e) => setSelectedModel(e.target.value)} className="bg-[#050508] border border-gray-700 rounded-lg px-3 py-1.5 text-sm text-gray-300">
            {models.map(m => (<option key={m.id} value={m.id} disabled={!m.available}>{m.name} {!m.available && '(No Key)'}</option>))}
          </select>
        </div>
        <div ref={chatContainerRef} className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-4 space-y-4">
          {messages.map((msg, i) => (
            <div key={i} className={`flex ${msg.role === 'user' ? 'justify-end' : 'justify-start'}`}>
              <div className={`max-w-[80%] rounded-2xl px-4 py-3 ${msg.role === 'user' ? 'bg-purple-600 text-white' : 'bg-[#0a0a0f] border border-gray-800 text-gray-200'}`}>
                <p className="whitespace-pre-wrap">{msg.content}</p>
                {msg.model && <p className="text-xs mt-2 opacity-50">via {msg.model} {msg.model.includes('ollama') ? '🏠' : '☁️'}</p>}
              </div>
            </div>
          ))}
          {isLoading && <div className="flex justify-start"><div className="bg-[#0a0a0f] border border-gray-800 rounded-2xl px-4 py-3 text-gray-400">Thinking...</div></div>}
          <div ref={messagesEndRef} />
        </div>
        <div className="p-4 bg-[#0a0a0f] border-t border-gray-800">
          <div className="flex items-end gap-3">
            <textarea 
              ref={textareaRef}
              value={input} 
              onChange={(e) => setInput(e.target.value)} 
              onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) { e.preventDefault(); sendMessage(); }}} 
              placeholder="Message MAX... (Shift+Enter for new line)" 
              rows={1}
              className="flex-1 bg-[#050508] border border-gray-700 rounded-xl px-4 py-3 text-white placeholder-gray-500 focus:border-purple-500 focus:outline-none resize-none overflow-y-auto"
              style={{ minHeight: '48px' }}
            />
            <button onClick={sendMessage} disabled={isLoading || !input.trim()} className="w-12 h-12 rounded-xl bg-purple-600 hover:bg-purple-500 disabled:bg-gray-700 flex items-center justify-center text-white font-bold shrink-0">→</button>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
EOF
echo "✅ Updated! Refresh browser."


Changes:

Added textareaRef and chatContainerRef
Auto-resize effect calculates 50% max of chat area
Starts small (1 row), grows as you type
items-end keeps send button at bottom
resize-none prevents manual drag (auto only)

content = r'''\'use client';

import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from 'react';

const API_URL = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://localhost:8000/api/v1';

interface Message {
  role: 'user' | 'assistant';
  content: string;
  model?: string;
}

interface Desk {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  status: string;
  stats: { completed: number; failed: number };
}

interface AIModel {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  available: boolean;
  type: string;
}

interface SystemStatus {
  backend: string;
  ollama: string;
  internet: boolean;
}

export default function FounderDashboard() {
  const [messages, setMessages] = useState<Message[]>([
    { role: 'assistant', content: "Hello! I'm MAX, your AI Assistant Manager. All 8 desks are online. How can I help?" }
  ]);
  const [input, setInput] = useState('');
  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false);
  const [desks, setDesks] = useState<Desk[]>([]);
  const [models, setModels] = useState<AIModel[]>([]);
  const [selectedModel, setSelectedModel] = useState('claude');
  const [stats, setStats] = useState({ total_completed: 0, active_tasks: 0 });
  const [systemStatus, setSystemStatus] = useState<SystemStatus>({ backend: '...', ollama: '...', internet: true });
  const messagesEndRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
  const textareaRef = useRef<HTMLTextAreaElement>(null);
  const chatContainerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchDesks();
    fetchModels();
    fetchStats();
    checkSystemStatus();
    const interval = setInterval(() => { fetchDesks(); fetchStats(); checkSystemStatus(); }, 30000);
    return () => clearInterval(interval);
  }, []);

  useEffect(() => {
    messagesEndRef.current?.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth' });
  }, [messages]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (textareaRef.current && chatContainerRef.current) {
      const textarea = textareaRef.current;
      const chatContainer = chatContainerRef.current;
      textarea.style.height = 'auto';
      const maxHeight = chatContainer.clientHeight * 0.5;
      const minHeight = 48;
      const newHeight = Math.min(Math.max(textarea.scrollHeight, minHeight), maxHeight);
      textarea.style.height = newHeight + 'px';
    }
  }, [input]);

  const fetchDesks = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(API_URL + '/max/desks');
      const data = await res.json();
      setDesks(data.desks || []);
    } catch (e) { console.error('Failed to fetch desks:', e); }
  };

  const fetchModels = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(API_URL + '/max/models');
      const data = await res.json();
      setModels(data.models || []);
    } catch (e) { console.error('Failed to fetch models:', e); }
  };

  const fetchStats = async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(API_URL + '/max/stats');
      const data = await res.json();
      setStats(data.stats || {});
    } catch (e) { console.error('Failed to fetch stats:', e); }
  };

  const checkSystemStatus = async () => {
    try {
      const backendRes = await fetch(API_URL.replace('/api/v1', '') + '/health');
      const backendOk = backendRes.ok ? '200' : 'ERR';
      let ollamaOk = 'OFF';
      try {
        const ollamaRes = await fetch('http://localhost:11434/api/tags');
        ollamaOk = ollamaRes.ok ? '200' : 'ERR';
      } catch { ollamaOk = 'OFF'; }
      setSystemStatus({ backend: backendOk, ollama: ollamaOk, internet: true });
    } catch (e) {
      setSystemStatus({ backend: 'ERR', ollama: 'ERR', internet: false });
    }
  };

  const sendMessage = async () => {
    if (!input.trim() || isLoading) return;
    const userMessage: Message = { role: 'user', content: input };
    setMessages(prev => [...prev, userMessage]);
    setInput('');
    setIsLoading(true);
    try {
      const res = await fetch(API_URL + '/max/chat', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ message: input, model: selectedModel, history: messages.map(m => ({ role: m.role, content: m.content })) })
      });
      const data = await res.json();
      const modelUsed = data.model_used || selectedModel;
      const isOffline = modelUsed.includes('ollama');
      if (isOffline && systemStatus.internet) {
        setSystemStatus(prev => ({ ...prev, internet: false }));
      }
      setMessages(prev => [...prev, { role: 'assistant', content: data.response || 'Error processing request.', model: modelUsed }]);
    } catch (e) {
      setMessages(prev => [...prev, { role: 'assistant', content: 'Connection error. Check if backend is running.' }]);
    } finally { setIsLoading(false); }
  };

  const getStatusColor = (status: string) => status === 'idle' ? 'bg-green-500' : status === 'busy' ? 'bg-yellow-500' : 'bg-gray-500';
  const getStatusDot = (status: string) => status === '200' ? 'bg-green-500' : status === 'OFF' ? 'bg-yellow-500' : 'bg-red-500';

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen bg-[#050508] flex">
      <div className="w-72 bg-[#0a0a0f] border-r border-gray-800 flex flex-col">
        <div className="p-4 border-b border-gray-800">
          <h1 className="text-xl font-bold text-purple-400">EmpireBox</h1>
          <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">Founder Edition</p>
        </div>
        <div className="p-3 border-b border-gray-800">
          <h3 className="text-xs font-semibold text-gray-500 uppercase mb-2">System Status</h3>
          <div className="space-y-1 text-xs">
            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
              <span className="text-gray-400">Backend</span>
              <div className="flex items-center gap-1"><div className={`w-2 h-2 rounded-full ${getStatusDot(systemStatus.backend)}`} /><span className="text-gray-500">{systemStatus.backend}</span></div>
            </div>
            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
              <span className="text-gray-400">Ollama</span>
              <div className="flex items-center gap-1"><div className={`w-2 h-2 rounded-full ${getStatusDot(systemStatus.ollama)}`} /><span className="text-gray-500">{systemStatus.ollama}</span></div>
            </div>
            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
              <span className="text-gray-400">Internet</span>
              <div className="flex items-center gap-1"><div className={`w-2 h-2 rounded-full ${systemStatus.internet ? 'bg-green-500' : 'bg-red-500'}`} /><span className="text-gray-500">{systemStatus.internet ? 'Online' : 'Offline'}</span></div>
            </div>
          </div>
          {!systemStatus.internet && <div className="mt-2 p-2 bg-yellow-900/30 border border-yellow-700 rounded text-xs text-yellow-400">Using Ollama fallback</div>}
        </div>
        <div className="p-3 grid grid-cols-2 gap-2">
          <div className="bg-[#050508] rounded-lg p-3 border border-gray-800"><span className="text-xs text-gray-400">Completed</span><p className="text-xl font-bold text-white">{stats.total_completed}</p></div>
          <div className="bg-[#050508] rounded-lg p-3 border border-gray-800"><span className="text-xs text-gray-400">Active</span><p className="text-xl font-bold text-white">{stats.active_tasks}</p></div>
        </div>
        <div className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-3">
          <h3 className="text-xs font-semibold text-gray-500 uppercase mb-2 px-2">AI Desks</h3>
          {desks.map(desk => (
            <div key={desk.id} className="flex items-center gap-3 p-2 rounded-lg hover:bg-[#050508] cursor-pointer">
              <div className="relative">
                <div className="w-8 h-8 rounded-lg bg-gray-800 flex items-center justify-center text-gray-400 text-sm">{desk.name.charAt(0)}</div>
                <div className={`absolute -bottom-0.5 -right-0.5 w-2.5 h-2.5 rounded-full border-2 border-[#0a0a0f] ${getStatusColor(desk.status)}`} />
              </div>
              <div className="flex-1"><p className="text-sm font-medium text-gray-200">{desk.name}</p><p className="text-xs text-gray-500">{desk.description}</p></div>
            </div>
          ))}
        </div>
      </div>
      <div className="flex-1 flex flex-col">
        <div className="h-14 bg-[#0a0a0f] border-b border-gray-800 flex items-center justify-between px-4">
          <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
            <div className="w-8 h-8 rounded-full bg-purple-600 flex items-center justify-center text-white font-bold">M</div>
            <div><h2 className="font-semibold text-white">MAX</h2><p className="text-xs text-gray-400">AI Assistant Manager</p></div>
          </div>
          <select value={selectedModel} onChange={(e) => setSelectedModel(e.target.value)} className="bg-[#050508] border border-gray-700 rounded-lg px-3 py-1.5 text-sm text-gray-300">
            {models.map(m => (<option key={m.id} value={m.id} disabled={!m.available}>{m.name} {!m.available && '(No Key)'}</option>))}
          </select>
        </div>
        <div ref={chatContainerRef} className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-4 space-y-4">
          {messages.map((msg, i) => (
            <div key={i} className={`flex ${msg.role === 'user' ? 'justify-end' : 'justify-start'}`}>
              <div className={`max-w-[80%] rounded-2xl px-4 py-3 ${msg.role === 'user' ? 'bg-purple-600 text-white' : 'bg-[#0a0a0f] border border-gray-800 text-gray-200'}`}>
                <p className="whitespace-pre-wrap">{msg.content}</p>
                {msg.model && <p className="text-xs mt-2 opacity-50">via {msg.model} {msg.model.includes('ollama') ? '🏠' : '☁️'}</p>}
              </div>
            </div>
          ))}
          {isLoading && <div className="flex justify-start"><div className="bg-[#0a0a0f] border border-gray-800 rounded-2xl px-4 py-3 text-gray-400">Thinking...</div></div>}
          <div ref={messagesEndRef} />
        </div>
        <div className="p-4 bg-[#0a0a0f] border-t border-gray-800">
          <div className="flex items-end gap-3">
            <textarea ref={textareaRef} value={input} onChange={(e) => setInput(e.target.value)} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) { e.preventDefault(); sendMessage(); }}} placeholder="Message MAX... (Shift+Enter for new line)" rows={1} className="flex-1 bg-[#050508] border border-gray-700 rounded-xl px-4 py-3 text-white placeholder-gray-500 focus:border-purple-500 focus:outline-none resize-none overflow-y-auto" style={{minHeight: '48px'}} />
            <button onClick={sendMessage} disabled={isLoading || !input.trim()} className="w-12 h-12 rounded-xl bg-purple-600 hover:bg-purple-500 disabled:bg-gray-700 flex items-center justify-center text-white font-bold shrink-0">→</button>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
'''[1:]

The shell is still truncating. Let me try a different approach - create the file in small chunks:

bash
